task_completion.description: Navigate to and open the Partial Correlation analysis in Jamovi.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Jamovi interface shows the Partial Correlation analysis panel open (usually under Regression -> Partial Correlation).

task_completion.description: Select 'Exam' and 'Anxiety' as variables, and 'Revise' as the control variable.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the Jamovi analysis setup panel, 'Exam' and 'Anxiety' are placed in the 'Variables' box, and 'Revise' is placed in the 'Controlling for' box.

task_completion.description: Generate the Partial Correlation results in Jamovi.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Jamovi results panel displays a Partial Correlation table showing a correlation coefficient (r) around -0.247 and a p-value around 0.012.
A save dialog in Jamovi or a file manager window shows the file 'ExamAnxiety_PartialCorr.omv' being saved or existing in the '/home/ga/Documents/Jamovi/' directory.

task_completion.description: Create the required text report with the correct results.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor shows 'partial_correlation_report.txt' containing exactly three lines: 'Partial r: -0.247' (or similar within +/- 0.1), 'P-value: 0.012' (or similar), and 'Significant: Yes'.

integrity.description: The agent used the Jamovi graphical interface to perform the analysis.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the agent interacting with Jamovi menus and dialogs rather than using terminal commands or Python scripts to bypass the UI.

integrity.description: The results in the text report match the results generated in Jamovi.
integrity.visual_evidence: The values typed into the text report correspond to the actual values visible in the Jamovi results panel in the screenshots.

integrity.description: The agent manually saved the files using standard GUI tools or basic terminal editors.
integrity.visual_evidence: The agent is seen using a text editor (like gedit, nano, or mousepad) to write the report and the Jamovi 'Save As' dialog to save the .omv file, rather than running a pre-written script to generate them.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The expected partial correlation coefficient (r) is -0.247 (acceptable range: -0.347 to -0.147). The expected p-value is 0.012, making the result statistically significant ('Yes'). The text report must contain exactly three lines formatted as 'Partial r: [value]', 'P-value: [value]', and 'Significant: Yes'. The Jamovi project must be saved as 'ExamAnxiety_PartialCorr.omv' in '/home/ga/Documents/Jamovi/'.
